metadata.description: Frame time benchmark - measuring how long each frame takes from start to flush

methodology.nocterm: Measures total frame duration using FrameTiming callback (build + layout + paint + diff + terminal flush). Frame rate limiting disabled. Uses Stopwatch and SchedulerBinding.addFrameTimingCallback().
methodology.ink: Measures time between consecutive render cycles using performance.now(). Uses setImmediate() to trigger maximum render rate.

nocterm.notes: Frame timing breakdown: Build (32µs) + Layout (31µs) + Paint (54µs) + Diff/Flush (~20µs) = ~137µs total

ink.notes: Measures frame-to-frame time including Node.js event loop. Ink uses React reconciler + Yoga layout.

fps.notes: Ink achieves ~21% more frames per second in this tight loop benchmark

frame_time_us.notes: Both frameworks render frames well under 16.67ms (60fps target = 16667µs)

interpretation: Both frameworks can easily achieve 60+ FPS (target: 16.67ms per frame)
Nocterm: 137µs average = capable of ~7300 FPS = 122x faster than 60fps requirement
Ink: 115µs average = capable of ~8700 FPS = 145x faster than 60fps requirement
The difference is likely due to measurement methodology - Ink measures frame-to-frame time including event loop overhead
Nocterm measures actual frame processing time (build+layout+paint+diff+flush)
For practical purposes, both are more than fast enough for any TUI application
